verdict_reasoning: B4GALT1 (beta-1,4-galactosyltransferase 1) is a type II single-pass transmembrane protein resident predominantly in the trans-Golgi network, where it functions in glycan biosynthesis. However, it is well-documented to have a dual localization: a pool is constitutively present on the cell surface (plasma membrane) across many somatic cell types, including epithelial and hematopoietic cells, where its catalytic domain faces outward as an ecto-enzyme. Additionally, lactation-specific association with alpha-lactalbumin in mammary gland modifies its substrate specificity. The surface pool has been detected by cell-surface biotinylation and flow cytometry. The dominant compartment is Golgi/endomembrane, but a real, documented minority PM pool exists via secretory cycling, placing this in dual_localization. A soluble secreted form also exists (shed ectodomain). Clinical utility as an accessible ecto-enzyme on tumor cells has also been noted.
